Abkhazia: A partially recognized country, it has received recognition from UN member states: Russia, Nicaragua, Venezuela, Nauru, and Syria, as well as from the partially recognized South Ossetia and the unrecognized Pridnestrovian Moldavian Republic (Transnistria).
Kosovo: As of now, the Republic of Kosovo is recognized by only 108 out of 193 UN member states.
Northern Cyprus: Considered unrecognized, as it has received diplomatic recognition only from Turkey.
South Ossetia: A partially recognized country, it has received recognition from UN member states: Russia, Nicaragua, Venezuela, Nauru, Syria, as well as the partially recognized Republic of Abkhazia and the Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic, and the unrecognized Pridnestrovian Moldavian Republic (Transnistria).
Transnistria: Considered an unrecognized country, even though it has received diplomatic recognition from Abkhazia and South Ossetia, since those countries themselves are recognized by very few others.
